name: Coleman v. Thompson
summary: The Court held that federal courts generally will not review claims not properly raised in state court unless the petitioner can show cause and prejudice for the default or fundamental miscarriage of justice.
significance: Establishes principles of procedural default in habeas review, holding that failure to comply with state procedural rules may bar federal review; relevant to the respondent's argument that Pitchford's trial-level procedural default is binding.
